study.name: D7960C00015 (AZD0780) — ASCVD / cardiovascular outcomes
study.indication: Established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD), or high risk of a first ASCVD event — MACE prevention
study.drug: AZD0780 — first-in-class ORAL PCSK9 inhibitor, 30 mg tablet once daily, on top of standard lipid-lowering therapy (vs matching placebo)

knowledgeBank.General Study Information: This is a Phase III study testing whether AZD0780 — an investigational first-in-class ORAL PCSK9 inhibitor taken as a 30 mg tablet once daily — lowers the risk of major heart problems (heart attack, stroke, and related events) in people who already have, or are at high risk for, hardening/blockage of the arteries (atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease). AZD0780 is taken on top of your usual cholesterol-lowering medicine (such as a statin); it is compared against a placebo (a tablet with no active drug). Neither you nor the study doctor will know which one you receive.
knowledgeBank.Trial Design: Randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group. Participants are randomly assigned to AZD0780 30 mg once daily or matching placebo, on top of standard-of-care lipid-lowering therapy. Screening is up to 3 weeks (may extend to 6 weeks if menopausal status must be confirmed). Visits occur at screening, randomisation, weeks 2/4/12/24/36/52, then about every 26 weeks. This is an event-driven study — it continues until enough cardiovascular events have occurred, with a median follow-up of at least ~3.1 years (study duration to primary analysis ~54 months).
knowledgeBank.Compensation / Reimbursement: Reimbursement is per completed visit: $150 for Visit 1 (Screening) and $150 for Visit 2 (Randomisation); $100 for each subsequent visit (Week 2, Weeks 4 & 12, Weeks 24/36/52, every-26-week visits thereafter, study closure, and qualifying unscheduled visits). This works out to roughly $900 over the first year, then about $100 per visit (~$200/year) for the multi-year follow-up. You are reimbursed after each completed visit even if you do not finish the study.
knowledgeBank.Blinding: Double-blind — both the participant and the study staff are blinded to whether the participant receives AZD0780 or placebo. AZD0780 and placebo are supplied as matching tablets.

// classification: verification_method ∈ {self_report,exam,lab,imaging,records,derived}; knockout_strength ∈ {hard,soft,none}; phone_screenable = self_report && hard. This is a cardiovascular outcomes trial — most INCLUSION criteria are lab/imaging (LDL-C, Lp(a), hs-CRP, CAC score, angiography, ABI, eGFR) and are confirmed at the screening VISIT, not on the phone. The phone pre-screen confirms patient-knowable qualifiers (age, prior heart attack/stroke/artery procedure, on a statin) and the clean self-reportable exclusions (cancer, transplant, substance abuse, pregnancy).

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Age: participants with a history of an ASCVD event must be ≥18 years at ICF signing; participants at increased risk of a FIRST ASCVD event must be male ≥50 or female ≥55 years.
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Age is patient-known. Phone gate uses the absolute minimum (≥18); the sex-specific 50/55 threshold for the primary-prevention arm is confirmed at the visit alongside the arm determination.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Qualifying ASCVD event/disease: history of MI or ischaemic stroke due to atherosclerosis ≥1 month prior to randomisation, or revascularisation for symptomatic lower-limb PAD; OR (primary-prevention arm) diagnostic evidence of significant atherosclerosis.
inclusionCriteria.rationale: A prior heart attack, stroke, or artery procedure is patient-known. Worded broadly on the phone to also capture primary-prevention patients with diagnosed artery disease; the precise arm + qualifying-event adjudication happens at the visit.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Primary-prevention arm: diagnostic evidence of atherosclerosis — CAC score ≥300 (or ≥100 with risk factors), obstructive CAD on angiography, ABI ≤0.85/0.9, carotid IMT >1mm, or polyvascular disease.
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Imaging/angiography read — screening-visit data, not phone-answerable.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: LDL-C threshold: ≥60 mg/dL (≥1.55 mmol/L) for the ASCVD-history arm; ≥100 mg/dL (≥2.6 mmol/L) for the primary-prevention arm.
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Central-lab LDL-C — confirmed at screening, not phone.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Additional risk factors (combinations required): Lp(a) ≥50 nmol/L, hs-CRP ≥2.0 mg/L, eGFR <60/≤45, polyvascular disease, prior amputation, recurrent events, current tobacco use, age ≥65.
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Lab/imaging combinations adjudicated at the visit; tobacco use is captured but is only one of several OR factors, not a standalone phone knockout.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: T2DM requiring ongoing medical therapy (a qualifying risk factor / diabetes pathway in the primary-prevention arm with end-organ disease).
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Diabetes is patient-known, but here it is one of several OR qualifiers (not a standalone hard knockout) and the end-organ-disease detail is visit/records-confirmed. Captured at the visit.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Stable background lipid-lowering therapy >28 days before screening — high-intensity statin, or lower-intensity statin plus ezetimibe/bempedoic acid, or maximally tolerated regimen (documented statin intolerance permitted).
inclusionCriteria.rationale: Statins/ezetimibe are common, patient-known brand medicines. Being on a cholesterol-lowering regimen is required; phone-answerable. Documented-intolerance exceptions are resolved at the visit.

inclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Female participants of childbearing potential who are sexually active must use a highly effective contraception method plus a barrier method through the study.
Capable of giving signed informed consent and willing to comply with study procedures.
Provision of signed Optional Genomics Initiative consent (optional).

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Underlying disease/condition (incl. homozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia, or LDL/plasma apheresis within 12 months) that may interfere with interpretation, per investigator.
Any revascularisation procedure planned within the next 3 months.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Patient may know of a planned procedure, but timing/eligibility impact is adjudicated at the visit; not a clean phone knockout.

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Imaging within last 3 years showing coronary calcium score of zero or CCTA with no atherosclerosis.
Calculated eGFR <15 mL/min/1.73 m2 at screening.
Uncontrolled severe hypertension: SBP >160 or DBP >110 mmHg at randomisation despite therapy.
Severe concomitant non-CV disease with estimated life expectancy <2 years.
Malignancy (except non-melanoma skin cancer, cervical in-situ carcinoma) within the last 3 years.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Recent cancer is patient-known and a clean exclusion knockout.

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Lab deviations at screening: AST/ALT >3×ULN, TBL >2×ULN, fasting triglycerides ≥400 mg/dL, creatine kinase >5×ULN, urine albumin/creatinine ≥500 mg/g.
Currently pregnant (confirmed positive test) or breast-feeding.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Patient-known; gated to female by routing; confirmed by test at the visit, so deferred if unasked on the phone.

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Known history of alcohol and/or drug abuse within the last 5 years.
Recipient of any major organ transplant (e.g., lung, liver, heart, bone marrow, renal).
History of hypersensitivity to drugs of similar chemical structure, or known prior intolerance to AZD0780.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Patient cannot self-report intolerance to the investigational study drug they have never taken (self-knowledge limit).

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Uncontrolled/serious disease incl. known major active infection (e.g., hepatitis B/C) or major organ-system dysfunction, per investigator.
Uncontrolled T2DM defined as HbA1c ≥9.5% at screening.
Inadequately treated hypothyroidism (TSH >1.5×ULN at screening) or thyroid replacement initiated/modified within last 3 months.
Use of mipomersen or lomitapide within 12 months of screening or planned use.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Specific advanced lipid-drug recall — confirmed from records at screening, not a reliable phone knockout.

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Use of gemfibrozil within one week prior to screening or planned use.
Use of PCSK9 inhibitors (evolocumab/alirocumab within 12 weeks, inclisiran within 18 months) or other PCSK9 inhibitor within 5 half-lives, or planned use.
exclusionCriteria.rationale: Specific advanced biologic recall — confirmed from records at screening, not a phone knockout.

exclusionCriteria.criterion_text: Participation in AZD0780 studies D7960C00012/00013, or another IMP within 30 days or 5 half-lives of screening.
Planned use of other investigational products/devices during the study.
Any condition that could interfere with study conduct, incl. inability to communicate/comply, per investigator.
Previous randomisation into the present study.
Acute ischaemic ASCVD event within 7 days prior to randomisation.
QTcF >500 msec at randomisation (absent conduction delay/BBB/pacing).
Sustained spontaneous ventricular arrhythmia requiring treatment within 30 days without ICD in place.

screeningQuestions.sms_question: What is your sex assigned at birth?

Has a doctor told you that you have heart or blood-vessel disease — for example a past heart attack or stroke, blocked or narrowed arteries, or a procedure such as a stent, bypass, or surgery on the arteries of your heart, neck, or legs?
Are you currently taking a cholesterol-lowering medicine, such as a statin (for example Lipitor, Crestor, Zocor, atorvastatin, rosuvastatin) or ezetimibe (Zetia)?
In the past 3 years, have you been diagnosed with or treated for cancer? (Minor skin cancers and cervical carcinoma-in-situ don't count — please answer no for those.)
Have you ever received a major organ transplant, such as a heart, liver, lung, kidney, or bone-marrow transplant?
In the last 5 years, have you had a problem with alcohol or drug abuse?
Are you currently pregnant or breastfeeding?
screeningQuestions.defer_note: Pregnancy is confirmed by test at the screening visit; if unasked on the phone it does not block pre-qualification. A disclosed 'yes' still disqualifies.

nodes.label: Are you interested in this study?
Diagnosed heart/blood-vessel disease or prior event/procedure?
Currently on a statin / cholesterol-lowering medicine?

refinedQuestions.allowed_exceptions: Eligibility by arm: ASCVD history ≥18; primary prevention male ≥50, female ≥55

refinedQuestions.text: What sex were you assigned at birth?
Has a doctor diagnosed you with heart or blood-vessel disease, like a heart attack, stroke, or artery surgery?
refinedQuestions.allowed_exceptions: Primary-prevention arm qualifies with diagnostic atherosclerosis instead of ASCVD history

refinedQuestions.text: Are you currently taking a statin or cholesterol medicine like Lipitor, Crestor, or Zetia?
refinedQuestions.allowed_exceptions: Must have been on stable therapy ≥28 days before screening

refinedQuestions.text: In the past 3 years, have you had cancer? (Not counting minor skin cancers or cervical carcinoma-in-situ.)
refinedQuestions.allowed_exceptions: Minor skin cancers and cervical carcinoma-in-situ do not count as disqualifying

refinedQuestions.text: Have you ever had a major organ transplant like a heart, liver, lung, kidney, or bone marrow?
In the last 5 years, have you had a problem with alcohol or drug abuse?
Are you currently pregnant or breastfeeding?
